PCB Design · Embedded Systems · Hardware Verification

2nd year Electrical Engineering student at UBC Okanagan. I design real PCBs, debug hardware with oscilloscopes, and build systems that work — from schematic to soldered board. Currently on the electrical subteam at UBCO Marine Robotics.

Projects

Engineering Work

PCB Design · Marine Robotics

4-Layer Actuator Control PCB

Designed and routed a 4-layer PCB for the actuator control system of UBCO Marine Robotics. Handled servo headers, power distribution, STM32-based MCU integration, and DRC/ERC verification in Altium Designer.

Altium Designer4-Layer RoutingSTM32DRC/ERC
Schematic Design · Power Electronics

Buck Converter Power Supply Schematic

Designed dual-output buck converter circuits using AP62300 regulators, targeting 6V and 6.8V rails for servo and logic power domains. Included bootstrap capacitors, feedback networks, and test points for bring-up verification.

Schematic CaptureBuck ConverterAP62300Power Design
Robotics · Embedded Systems

Sustainable Folding E-Bike — APSC 171

Electrical sub-team member on a folding e-bike design project. Integrated RC remote control system, verified steering and throttle functionality, and wired electrical components within frame constraints.

Embedded CRC IntegrationWiring LayoutSolidWorks
PCB Assembly · Lab Work

PCB Bring-Up & SMD Assembly

Assembled and soldered PCB prototypes for UBCO Marine Robotics, performing SMD component placement. Conducted power-on testing and signal verification using multimeter and oscilloscope to validate board functionality.

SMD SolderingPCB AssemblyPower-On TestingMultimeter
Hardware Verification · Lab Work

Signal Verification & Debugging

Used a Tektronix TDS 2012B oscilloscope throughout 1st and 2nd year labs and PCB bring-up sessions — measuring waveforms, verifying signal timing, and checking voltages during hardware testing and debugging.

OscilloscopeSignal AnalysisHardware TestingPCB Bring-up
